---
title: Group 15 -- HDB Resale Price Analysis
subtitle: AAI1001 -- Data Engineering and Visualization 
author: Group 15
date: 2026-01-19
date-format: "dddd MMM DD, YYYY"

toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
number-depth: 2
engine: knitr
format:
  revealjs:
    self-contained: true
    embed-resources: true
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    margin: 0.05
    min-scale: 0.2
    max-scale: 2.0
    scrollable: true
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: false
    incremental: true
    logo: imgs/sit_logo_mark.png
    footer:  AAI1001 -- AY25/26 Tri 2 (Assisted by GenAI)
    html-table-processing: none
    code-block-height: 700px
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(knitr)
library(gt)
library(tidyverse)
```

## Why Study HDB Resale Prices? (Daryl)

<hr>

- HDB housing forms the primary housing market in Singapore  
- Resale transactions reflect real market demand  
- Price variation affects affordability and long-term financial planning  
- However, most public discussions focus on headline prices

## Limitations of Headline Price (Daryl)

<hr>

- Median prices do not capture full price variability  
- Different flat types may exhibit different risk exposure  
- Extreme transactions influence financial risk perception  
- Distribution analysis provides deeper market insight

## Orignal Data Visualisation: Density Heatmap of Resale Prices (Daryl)

<hr>

![](images/visualisation.jpg){width=1100}

- Density heatmap visualising resale transactions across flat types  
- Colour intensity represents transaction frequency  
- Highlights clustering of prices within each housing category  
- Provides an intuitive overview of market pricing patterns

## Strengths of the Original Visulisation (Speaker 2)

<hr>

content

## Limitations of the Original Visulisation (Speaker 2)

<hr>

content

## Data Sources and Selection Rationale (Desmond)

<hr>

content

## Data Engineering Workflow (Desmond)

<hr>

content

## Feature Engineering and Aggregation (Desmond)

<hr>

content

## Proposed Visualisation Improvements (Speaker 4)

<hr>

content

## Improved Visualisation: Split Violin with Boxplot Overlay (Speaker 4)

<hr>

content

## Key Insights from the Improved Visualisation (Speaker 5)

<hr>

content

## Interpretation of Pricing Patterns

<hr>

content

## Conclusion and Implications

<hr>

content
